steps.title: Find the missing side using Pythagoras
steps.content:
The right angle is at $S$, so $TR$ is the hypotenuse with $TR = 79$ and leg $RS = \sqrt{157}$. Apply the Pythagorean theorem to find leg $TS$:
$$TS^2 = TR^2 - RS^2 = 79^2 - 157 = 6241 - 157 = 6084$$
$$TS = \sqrt{6084} = 78$$

steps.title: Compute the sine ratio
steps.content:
From angle $R$, the opposite side is $TS = 78$ and the hypotenuse is $TR = 79$:
$$\sin R = \frac{TS}{TR} = \frac{78}{79} \approx 0.9873$$
The answer is 78/79.
